structuredRootHelp:
Usage: privacy-pools [options] [command]

Privacy Pools: a compliant way to transact privately on Ethereum

Options:
  -V, --version        output the version number
  -c, --chain <name>   Target chain (mainnet, arbitrum, optimism, ...)
  -j, --json           Machine-readable JSON output on stdout
  --format <format>    Output format: table (default), csv, json (choices:
                       "table", "csv", "json")
  -y, --yes            Skip confirmation prompts
  -r, --rpc-url <url>  Override RPC URL
  --agent              Machine-friendly mode (alias for --json --yes --quiet)
  -q, --quiet          Suppress human-oriented stderr output
  -v, --verbose        Enable verbose/debug output
  --no-banner          Disable ASCII banner output
  --no-color           Disable colored output (also respects NO_COLOR env var)
  --timeout <seconds>  Network/transaction timeout in seconds (default: 30)
  -h, --help           display help for command

Commands:
  init                 Initialize wallet and configuration
  upgrade              Check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI
  flow                 Guided deposit-to-private-withdrawal workflow
  pools                List available pools and assets
  deposit              Deposit funds into a Privacy Pool
  accounts             List your Pool Accounts with balances
  migrate              Inspect legacy migration readiness on CLI-supported
                       chains
  withdraw             Privately withdraw funds via relayer
  ragequit|exit        Publicly recover funds to your original deposit address (self-custody guarantee)
  history              Show chronological event history (deposits, migrations,
                       withdrawals, ragequits)
  sync                 Force-sync local account state from onchain events
  status               Show configuration and check connection health
  activity             Show public activity feed
  stats                Show public statistics
  guide                Show usage guide, workflow, and reference
  capabilities         Describe CLI capabilities for agent discovery
  describe             Describe one command for runtime agent introspection
  completion           Generate shell completion script
  help                 display help for command

  Get started:      privacy-pools init
  Full guide:       privacy-pools guide
  Command help:     privacy-pools <command> --help
  Agent discovery:  privacy-pools capabilities

helpTextByPath.init:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools init [options][22m

Initialize wallet and configuration


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m--recovery-phrase <phrase>[39m     Import an existing recovery phrase (unsafe:
                                 visible in process list)
  [33m--recovery-phrase-file <path>[39m  Import recovery phrase from a file (raw phrase
                                 or Privacy Pools backup file)
  [33m--recovery-phrase-stdin[39m        Import recovery phrase from stdin (raw phrase
                                 or Privacy Pools backup text)
  [33m--show-recovery-phrase[39m         Include generated recovery phrase in JSON
                                 output (unsafe: may be logged or piped)
  [33m--private-key <key>[39m            Set the signer private key (unsafe: visible in
                                 process list)
  [33m--private-key-file <path>[39m      Set the signer private key from a file
  [33m--private-key-stdin[39m            Set the signer private key from stdin
  [33m--default-chain <chain>[39m        Set default chain
  [33m--rpc-url <url>[39m                Set RPC URL for the default chain
  [33m--force[39m                        Overwrite existing configuration without
                                 prompting
  [33m-h, --help[39m                     display help for command
Creates or imports the local Privacy Pools wallet state under ~/.privacy-pools/. The recovery phrase controls deposit privacy and account restoration, while the signer key pays gas and submits transactions; they are intentionally separate secrets.
When you generate a fresh wallet, the CLI uses a 24-word recovery phrase. Imported recovery phrases may be either 12 or 24 words. Back up the recovery phrase immediately: without it, deposited funds cannot be restored.
Zero-knowledge proof generation uses bundled checksum-verified circuit artifacts shipped with the CLI package. Set PRIVACY_POOLS_CIRCUITS_DIR only when you intentionally want to override that packaged directory with a pre-provisioned one.

Examples:
  privacy-pools init
  privacy-pools init --yes --default-chain mainnet
  privacy-pools init --force --yes --default-chain mainnet
  privacy-pools init --agent --default-chain mainnet --show-recovery-phrase
  privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file ./my-recovery-phrase.txt --private-key-file ./my-key.txt
  cat phrase.txt | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-stdin --yes --default-chain mainnet
  printf '%s\n' 0x... | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file ./my-recovery-phrase.txt --private-key-stdin --yes --default-chain mainnet

Safety notes:
  The recovery phrase and signer key are independent secrets: the phrase controls deposit privacy, the key pays gas. Neither is derived from the other.
  Newly generated recovery phrases use 24 words for stronger security. Imported recovery phrases may still be 12 or 24 words.
  Legacy pre-upgrade accounts may need website migration or website-based recovery before the CLI can safely restore them.

Agent workflow:
  When generating a new recovery phrase in machine mode, pass --show-recovery-phrase and capture it immediately.
  When importing an existing recovery phrase, nextActions points to migrate status --agent --all-chains first to check for existing deposits across all chains before transacting.

JSON output (--json):
  { defaultChain, signerKeySet, recoveryPhraseRedacted? | recoveryPhrase?, warning?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
helpTextByPath.upgrade:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools upgrade [options][22m

Check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m--check[39m     Check npm for a newer privacy-pools-cli release without installing
              it
  [33m-h, --help[39m  display help for command
Checks npm for the latest published privacy-pools-cli version and can upgrade a supported global npm install in place.
Automatic upgrade is supported only for recognized global npm installs. Source checkouts, non-npm global installs, local project installs, npx-style ephemeral runs, CI, and other ambiguous contexts never mutate; the CLI returns manual guidance plus an exact follow-up npm command.
Machine modes (--json / --agent) stay check-only unless --yes is also present.

Examples:
  privacy-pools upgrade --check
  privacy-pools upgrade
  privacy-pools upgrade --yes
  privacy-pools upgrade --agent --check
  privacy-pools upgrade --agent --yes

Safety notes:
  Automatic upgrade only runs for recognized global npm installs of privacy-pools-cli.
  Source checkouts, non-npm global installs, local project installs, npx-style ephemeral runs, CI, and ambiguous contexts stay read-only and still return an exact npm follow-up command.
  A successful upgrade updates the installed CLI on disk but does not hot-reexec the current process. Re-run privacy-pools after it completes.

Agent workflow:
  In machine modes, upgrade is check-only unless --yes is explicitly present.
  Treat status = ready as an available update on a supported global npm install, status = manual as an available update requiring manual follow-up, and status = upgraded as a completed install that still requires a fresh CLI invocation.

JSON output (--json):
  { mode: "upgrade", status, currentVersion, latestVersion, updateAvailable, performed, command|null, installContext: { kind, supportedAutoRun, reason }, installedVersion|null, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
helpTextByPath.flow:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools flow [options] [command][22m

Guided deposit-to-private-withdrawal workflow


  [1mExplore (no wallet needed)[22m
  [36mstatus [workflowId[39m[2m|latest][22m           Show the saved easy-path workflow state

  [1mTransact (run init first)[22m
  [36mragequit [workflowId[39m[2m|latest][22m         Recover a saved workflow publicly via
                                       ragequit

[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-h, --help[39m                           display help for command

Top-level namespace for the persisted easy path on top of the same public deposit, ASP review, and relayed private withdrawal flow used by the website and manual CLI commands.
`privacyDelayConfigured = false` in flow JSON means a legacy saved workflow was normalized to `off` without an explicitly saved privacy-delay policy.
Manual commands remain unchanged and are still the advanced/manual path when you need custom Pool Account selection, partial amounts, direct withdrawals, unsigned payloads, or dry-runs.

Examples:
  privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient...
  privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient... --watch
  privacy-pools flow start 100 USDC --to 0xRecipient... --new-wallet --export-new-wallet ./flow-wallet.txt
  privacy-pools flow watch
  privacy-pools flow status latest
  privacy-pools flow ragequit latest

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init for start/watch/ragequit; saved workflow for status
helpTextByPath.flow start:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools flow start [options] <amount> <asset>[22m

Deposit now and save a later private withdrawal workflow

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  [33mamount[39m                      Amount to deposit (e.g. 0.1)
  [33masset[39m                       Asset symbol (e.g. ETH, USDC)


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-t, --to <address>[39m          Recipient address for the later private withdrawal
  [33m--privacy-delay <profile>[39m   Privacy delay profile: off (no hold), balanced
                              (15-90m randomized), or aggressive (2-12h
                              randomized) (choices: "off", "balanced",
                              "aggressive")
  [33m--new-wallet[39m                Create and use a dedicated wallet for this
                              workflow
  [33m--export-new-wallet <path>[39m  Export the generated workflow wallet backup before
                              continuing (requires --new-wallet)
  [33m--watch[39m                     Keep watching this workflow until it finishes or
                              pauses
  [33m-h, --help[39m                  display help for command
This is the compressed happy-path command: it performs the normal public deposit, saves a workflow locally, and targets a later relayed private withdrawal (the relayer submits the withdrawal onchain) from that same Pool Account to the saved recipient.
With --new-wallet, the CLI generates a dedicated workflow wallet, waits for it to be funded, then continues automatically. ETH flows wait for the full ETH target; ERC20 flows wait for the token amount plus native ETH gas reserve.
The saved workflow always spends the full remaining balance from the newly created Pool Account. The recipient receives the net amount after relayer fees and any ERC20 extra-gas funding, and the workflow never auto-ragequits.

Examples:
  privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient...
  privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient... --privacy-delay off
  privacy-pools flow start 100 USDC --to 0xRecipient... --chain mainnet
  privacy-pools flow start 100 USDC --to 0xRecipient... --new-wallet --export-new-wallet ./flow-wallet.txt
  privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient... --watch --agent

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init

Safety notes:
  Deposits are always public on-chain. The ASP reviews the deposit before private withdrawal is possible.
  If --to is omitted in interactive mode, the CLI prompts for the recipient. In machine modes, --to remains required.
  In machine modes, non-round flow amounts are rejected. Use a round amount in agent/non-interactive runs, or switch to interactive mode if you intentionally accept that tradeoff.
  New workflows default to a balanced post-approval privacy delay before relayed withdrawal. off = no added hold, balanced = randomized 15 to 90 minutes, aggressive = randomized 2 to 12 hours.
  Vetting fees can turn a round deposit input into a non-round committed balance, so flow start may still emit an advisory amount-pattern warning for the later full-balance auto-withdrawal.
  flow start surfaces advisory privacy warnings when the saved workflow is configured to auto-withdraw a full non-round balance, or when timing delay is explicitly disabled.
  --export-new-wallet is only valid with --new-wallet.
  Non-interactive workflow wallets require --export-new-wallet so the generated private key is backed up before the flow starts.
  The generated workflow key is also stored locally under workflow-secrets until the workflow completes or recovers publicly, so --export-new-wallet is a backup copy rather than the only retained secret.
  Dedicated workflow wallets may retain leftover asset balance or gas reserve after paused or terminal states, so check them manually before assuming they are empty.
  The saved flow spends the entire remaining Pool Account balance, but the recipient receives the net amount after relayer fees and any ERC20 extra-gas funding.
  Manual commands remain the advanced/manual path when you need custom control over Pool Account selection, amount, or withdrawal mode.

Agent workflow:
  With --new-wallet, the flow stays attached automatically and waits for funding, deposit, approval, and withdrawal unless you detach with Ctrl-C.
  Use --watch to stay attached on configured-wallet workflows; otherwise the workflow is persisted locally and flow watch <workflowId> is the canonical resume path.

JSON output (--json):
  { mode: "flow", action: "start", workflowId, phase, walletMode, walletAddress|null, requiredNativeFunding|null, requiredTokenFunding|null, backupConfirmed?, chain, asset, depositAmount, recipient, poolAccountId|null, poolAccountNumber|null, depositTxHash|null, depositBlockNumber|null, depositExplorerUrl|null, committedValue|null, aspStatus?, privacyDelayProfile, privacyDelayConfigured, privacyDelayUntil|null, withdrawTxHash|null, withdrawBlockNumber|null, withdrawExplorerUrl|null, ragequitTxHash|null, ragequitBlockNumber|null, ragequitExplorerUrl|null, warnings?: [{ code, category: "privacy", message }], lastError?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
helpTextByPath.flow watch:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools flow watch [options] [workflowId|latest][22m

Resume a saved flow through funding, approval, privacy delay, and withdrawal

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  workflowId|latest          Saved workflow id or 'latest' (defaults to latest)


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m--privacy-delay <profile>[39m  Persist or override the saved privacy delay
                             profile: off (no hold), balanced (15-90m
                             randomized), or aggressive (2-12h randomized)
                             (choices: "off", "balanced", "aggressive")
  [33m-h, --help[39m                 display help for command
Re-checks a saved workflow using the same protocol realities as the frontend. It can resume dedicated-wallet funding, public deposit reconciliation, ASP review, privacy-delay waiting, relayed withdrawal, and pending receipt reconciliation.
Workflow phases include awaiting_funding, depositing_publicly, awaiting_asp, approved_waiting_privacy_delay, approved_ready_to_withdraw, withdrawing, completed, completed_public_recovery, paused_poa_required, paused_declined, and stopped_external.
The saved workflow phase is reported in phase, while the deposit review state from the ASP (the approval service) remains available separately in aspStatus.
When a saved workflow is using balanced or aggressive privacy delay, approval first transitions into approved_waiting_privacy_delay until the persisted randomized hold expires.
Ctrl-C detaches cleanly. It does not cancel the saved workflow or mutate it beyond any state that was already persisted.
flow watch is intentionally unbounded. Agents that need a wall-clock limit should wrap the command in their own external timeout.

Examples:
  privacy-pools flow watch
  privacy-pools flow watch latest --privacy-delay off   # updates the saved privacy-delay policy
  privacy-pools flow watch latest --agent
  privacy-pools flow watch 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init

Safety notes:
  Paused states are successful workflow states, not CLI errors. Declined workflows surface flow ragequit as the canonical public recovery path, and PoA-required workflows can either resume privately after the external Proof of Association step or recover publicly with flow ragequit.
  If the saved full-balance withdrawal falls below the relayer minimum, flow watch surfaces flow ragequit as the required public recovery path because saved flows only support relayed private withdrawals.
  Once the public deposit exists, operators can also choose flow ragequit manually instead of waiting, but it is not emitted as the default nextAction while the workflow is still progressing normally. The happy-path canonical resume command remains flow watch.
  Passing --privacy-delay on flow watch updates the saved workflow policy. off = no added hold, balanced = randomized 15 to 90 minutes, aggressive = randomized 2 to 12 hours.
  Switching to off clears any saved hold immediately; switching between balanced and aggressive resamples from the override time.

Agent workflow:
  New-wallet workflows wait for funding automatically. ERC20 workflows require both the token amount and a native ETH gas reserve in the generated wallet before the public deposit can proceed.
  When the saved Pool Account is approved, flow watch performs the relayed private withdrawal automatically using the saved recipient and the full remaining balance of that same Pool Account after any configured privacy delay hold expires.
  flow watch keeps polling until the saved workflow changes or finishes. If your automation should stop after a fixed duration, wrap the CLI call in your own external timeout.

JSON output (--json):
  { mode: "flow", action: "watch", workflowId, phase, walletMode, walletAddress|null, requiredNativeFunding|null, requiredTokenFunding|null, backupConfirmed?, chain, asset, depositAmount, recipient, poolAccountId|null, poolAccountNumber|null, depositTxHash|null, depositBlockNumber|null, depositExplorerUrl|null, committedValue|null, aspStatus?, privacyDelayProfile, privacyDelayConfigured, privacyDelayUntil|null, withdrawTxHash|null, withdrawBlockNumber|null, withdrawExplorerUrl|null, ragequitTxHash|null, ragequitBlockNumber|null, ragequitExplorerUrl|null, warnings?: [{ code, category: "privacy", message }], lastError?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
helpTextByPath.flow status:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools flow status [options] [workflowId|latest][22m

Show the saved easy-path workflow state

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  workflowId|latest  Saved workflow id or 'latest' (defaults to latest)


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-h, --help[39m         display help for command
Reads the persisted workflow snapshot and prints the current saved phase plus the canonical next action.
This is a saved local snapshot only. Run flow watch to re-check live state and advance the workflow.
When using latest, the CLI fails closed if unreadable saved workflow files could be newer than the latest readable workflow.
This is read-only and does not require init if the saved workflow already exists locally.

Examples:
  privacy-pools flow status
  privacy-pools flow status latest --agent
  privacy-pools flow status 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000

Prerequisites:
  Requires: saved workflow (usually created after init)

JSON output (--json):
  { mode: "flow", action: "status", workflowId, phase, walletMode, walletAddress|null, requiredNativeFunding|null, requiredTokenFunding|null, backupConfirmed?, chain, asset, depositAmount, recipient, poolAccountId|null, poolAccountNumber|null, depositTxHash|null, depositBlockNumber|null, depositExplorerUrl|null, committedValue|null, aspStatus?, privacyDelayProfile, privacyDelayConfigured, privacyDelayUntil|null, withdrawTxHash|null, withdrawBlockNumber|null, withdrawExplorerUrl|null, ragequitTxHash|null, ragequitBlockNumber|null, ragequitExplorerUrl|null, warnings?: [{ code, category: "privacy", message }], lastError?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
helpTextByPath.flow ragequit:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools flow ragequit [options] [workflowId|latest][22m

Recover a saved workflow publicly via ragequit

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  workflowId|latest  Saved workflow id or 'latest' (defaults to latest)


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-h, --help[39m         display help for command
Uses the saved workflow context to perform the public recovery path without changing any manual commands.
Once the public deposit exists, flow ragequit remains available as an optional public recovery path until the workflow reaches a terminal state. Declined flows use it as the canonical recovery path.
If a saved full-balance workflow can no longer satisfy the relayer minimum, flow ragequit becomes the required recovery path because the saved flow only supports relayed private withdrawal.
For workflow wallets, this uses the stored per-workflow private key. For configured-wallet workflows, it must use the original depositor signer that created the saved flow.

Examples:
  privacy-pools flow ragequit
  privacy-pools flow ragequit latest --agent
  privacy-pools flow ragequit 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init

Safety notes:
  This is a public recovery path. It exits to the original deposit address and does not preserve privacy.
  Configured-wallet recovery only works when the current signer still matches the original depositor address saved with the workflow.

JSON output (--json):
  { mode: "flow", action: "ragequit", workflowId, phase, walletMode, walletAddress|null, requiredNativeFunding|null, requiredTokenFunding|null, backupConfirmed?, chain, asset, depositAmount, recipient, poolAccountId|null, poolAccountNumber|null, depositTxHash|null, depositBlockNumber|null, depositExplorerUrl|null, committedValue|null, aspStatus?, privacyDelayProfile, privacyDelayConfigured, privacyDelayUntil|null, withdrawTxHash|null, withdrawBlockNumber|null, withdrawExplorerUrl|null, ragequitTxHash|null, ragequitBlockNumber|null, ragequitExplorerUrl|null, lastError?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
helpTextByPath.pools:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools pools [options] [asset][22m

List available pools and assets

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  [33masset[39m             Asset symbol for detail view (e.g. ETH, BOLD)


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m--all-chains[39m      Include testnet chains (mainnet chains shown by default)
  [33m--search <query>[39m  Filter by chain/symbol/address/scope
  [33m--sort <mode>[39m     Sort mode (default, asset-asc, asset-desc, tvl-desc,
                    tvl-asc, deposits-desc, deposits-asc, chain-asset) (choices:
                    "default", "asset-asc", "asset-desc", "tvl-desc", "tvl-asc",
                    "deposits-desc", "deposits-asc", "chain-asset", default:
                    "tvl-desc")
  [33m-h, --help[39m        display help for command
Lists the public Privacy Pools registry and asset metadata. By default, bare `pools` queries the CLI-supported mainnet chains together; pass --chain to scope a single network or --all-chains to include supported testnets too.

Examples:
  privacy-pools pools
  privacy-pools pools ETH
  privacy-pools pools BOLD --chain mainnet
  privacy-pools pools --all-chains --sort tvl-desc
  privacy-pools pools --search usdc --sort asset-asc
  privacy-pools pools --agent --chain mainnet

Agent workflow:
  In pools JSON, 'asset' is the symbol for CLI follow-up commands and 'tokenAddress' is the contract address.

JSON output (--json):
  { chain?, allChains?, chains?, search, sort, pools: [{ chain?, asset, tokenAddress, pool, scope, decimals, minimumDeposit, vettingFeeBPS, maxRelayFeeBPS, totalInPoolValue, totalInPoolValueUsd, totalDepositsValue, totalDepositsValueUsd, acceptedDepositsValue, acceptedDepositsValueUsd, pendingDepositsValue, pendingDepositsValueUsd, totalDepositsCount, acceptedDepositsCount, pendingDepositsCount, growth24h, pendingGrowth24h }], warnings?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
  detail (<asset>): { chain, asset, tokenAddress, pool, scope, ..., myFunds?, myFundsWarning?, recentActivity? }
  detail myFunds: { balance, usdValue, poolAccounts, pendingCount, poaRequiredCount, declinedCount, accounts: [{ id, status, aspStatus, value }] }

Show public statistics


  [36mglobal[39m          Show global Privacy Pools statistics (all-time and last 24h)
  [36mpool [options][39m  Show statistics for a specific pool (all-time and last 24h)
  [36mhelp [command][39m  display help for command

helpTextByPath.status:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools status [options][22m

Show configuration and check connection health


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m--check[39m      Run both RPC and ASP health checks
  [33m--no-check[39m   Suppress default health checks
  [33m--check-rpc[39m  Actively test RPC connectivity
  [33m--check-asp[39m  Actively test ASP liveness
  [33m-h, --help[39m   display help for command
Use recommendedMode plus blockingIssues[]/warnings[] for machine gating, and keep readyForDeposit/readyForWithdraw/readyForUnsigned as configuration capability flags only.
When status falls back to recommendedMode = read-only because RPC health is degraded, nextActions stays on public discovery and avoids account-state guidance until connectivity is restored.
When only the ASP is degraded but RPC is healthy, status still keeps nextActions on public discovery, while warning that public recovery remains available through ragequit or flow ragequit if the operator already knows the affected account or workflow.

Examples:
  privacy-pools status
  privacy-pools status --check
  privacy-pools status --no-check
  privacy-pools status --agent --check-rpc
  privacy-pools status --chain mainnet --rpc-url https://...

JSON output (--json):

helpTextByPath.deposit:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools deposit [options] <amount> [asset][22m

Deposit funds into a Privacy Pool

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  [33mamount[39m                        Amount to deposit (e.g. 0.1)
  [33masset[39m                         Asset symbol (e.g. ETH, USDC)


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-a, --asset <symbol|address>[39m  Asset to deposit (symbol like ETH, USDC, or
                                contract address)
  [33m--unsigned [format][39m           Build unsigned transaction without submitting;
                                format: envelope (default) or tx (choices:
                                "envelope", "tx")
  [33m--dry-run[39m                     Validate and preview the transaction without
                                submitting
  [33m--ignore-unique-amount[39m        Allow non-round deposit amounts (weaker privacy;
                                round amounts are harder to fingerprint)
  [33m-h, --help[39m                    display help for command
Builds the deposit transaction and submits it onchain. After install, the CLI uses bundled checksum-verified circuit artifacts for the local commitment precomputation path, so there is no runtime download step when proofs are needed.
Most proof-generation steps complete in roughly 10-30s once the packaged artifacts are verified locally.
In machine-oriented modes, non-round deposit amounts are rejected by default because they can fingerprint the deposit. Prefer round amounts unless you intentionally accept that privacy trade-off.

Examples:
  privacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH
  privacy-pools deposit 100 USDC
  privacy-pools deposit 0.05 ETH --agent
  privacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH --unsigned
  privacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH --dry-run
  privacy-pools deposit 0.1 --asset ETH --chain mainnet

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init

Safety notes:
  Deposits are reviewed by the ASP before approval. Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).
  A vetting fee is deducted from the deposit amount by the pool's ASP.
  Only approved deposits can use withdraw, whether relayed or direct. Declined deposits must use ragequit publicly. Deposits that require Proof of Association (PoA) must complete the PoA flow at tornado.0xbow.io before they can withdraw privately.

Agent workflow:
  Poll accounts --chain <chain> --pending-only while the Pool Account remains pending; when it disappears from pending results, re-run accounts --chain <chain> to confirm whether aspStatus became approved, declined, or requires Proof of Association. Withdraw only after approval; ragequit if declined; complete Proof of Association at tornado.0xbow.io first if needed. Always preserve the same --chain scope for both polling and confirmation.
  If the deposit transaction was submitted but confirmation timed out or the CLI was interrupted afterward, run sync --chain <chain> before retrying so local state can detect the onchain deposit.

JSON output (--json):
  { operation, txHash, amount, committedValue, asset, chain, poolAccountNumber, poolAccountId, poolAddress, scope, label, blockNumber, explorerUrl, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
  --unsigned: { mode, operation, chain, asset, amount, precommitment, transactions[] }
  --unsigned tx: [{ from, to, data, value, valueHex, chainId, description }]
  --dry-run: { dryRun, operation, chain, asset, amount, poolAccountNumber, poolAccountId, precommitment, balanceSufficient }

Additional modes:
  --unsigned builds transaction payloads without submitting.
  --dry-run validates the operation without submitting it.
helpTextByPath.withdraw:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools withdraw [options] [command] [amount] [asset][22m

Privately withdraw funds via relayer

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  [33mamount[39m                                    Amount to withdraw (e.g. 0.05, 50%)
  [33masset[39m                                     Asset symbol (e.g. ETH, USDC)


  quote [options] <amountOrAsset> [amount]  Request relayer quote and limits without generating a proof
[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-t, --to <address>[39m                        Recipient address (required for relayed)
  [33m-p, --from-pa <PA-#|#>[39m                    Withdraw from a specific Pool Account (e.g. PA-2)
  [33m--direct[39m                                  NOT recommended. Withdraw directly onchain, publicly linking deposit and withdrawal addresses. Use relayed mode (default) for privacy.
  [33m--unsigned [format][39m                       Build unsigned transaction without submitting; format: envelope (default) or tx (choices: "envelope", "tx")
  [33m--dry-run[39m                                 Generate and verify withdrawal artifacts without submitting
  [33m-a, --asset <symbol|address>[39m              Asset to withdraw
  [33m--all[39m                                     Withdraw entire Pool Account balance
  [33m--extra-gas[39m                               Request gas tokens with withdrawal (default: true for ERC20)
  [33m--no-extra-gas[39m                            Disable extra gas request
  [33m-h, --help[39m                                display help for command

Relayed withdrawal is the default because it preserves privacy and follows the website-style happy path. Direct withdrawal is still available, but it links the deposit and withdrawal onchain and should be treated as an explicit privacy trade-off.
Pool Accounts marked poa_required cannot withdraw privately until Proof of Association is completed at tornado.0xbow.io.
Like deposits, machine-oriented modes reject non-round amounts by default because unusual amounts can fingerprint the withdrawal. Opt out only when you intentionally accept that trade-off.

Examples:
  privacy-pools withdraw 0.05 ETH --to 0xRecipient...
  privacy-pools withdraw 0.05 ETH --to 0xRecipient... --from-pa PA-2
  privacy-pools withdraw --all ETH --to 0xRecipient...
  privacy-pools withdraw 50% ETH --to 0xRecipient...
  privacy-pools withdraw 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient... --dry-run
  privacy-pools withdraw quote 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient...
  privacy-pools withdraw 0.05 ETH --to 0xRecipient... --chain mainnet

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init (account state should be synced)

Safety notes:
  Always prefer relayed withdrawals (the default). Direct withdrawals (--direct) are NOT privacy-preserving: they publicly link your deposit address and withdrawal address onchain. Only use --direct if you understand and accept the privacy trade-off.
  ASP approval is required for both relayed and direct withdrawals. Declined deposits must ragequit publicly to the original deposit address.
  Relayed withdrawals must also respect the relayer minimum. If a withdrawal would leave a positive remainder below that minimum, the CLI warns so you can withdraw less, use --all/100%, or choose a public recovery path later.

Agent workflow:
  If the CLI is interrupted after proof generation but before submission completes, re-run withdraw to generate a fresh proof and re-evaluate the current account state.
  If a direct or relayed withdrawal transaction was submitted but confirmation timed out, run sync --chain <chain> before retrying so local state can detect the onchain result.

JSON output (--json):
  { operation, mode, txHash, blockNumber, amount, recipient, explorerUrl, poolAddress, scope, asset, chain, poolAccountNumber, poolAccountId, feeBPS, extraGas?, remainingBalance, anonymitySet?: { eligible, total, percentage }, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
  direct: same fields but mode: "direct", feeBPS: null, no extraGas, and human output explains the onchain link between deposit and withdrawal.
  quote: { mode: "relayed-quote", chain, asset, amount, recipient, minWithdrawAmount, minWithdrawAmountFormatted, baseFeeBPS, quoteFeeBPS, feeAmount, netAmount, feeCommitmentPresent, quoteExpiresAt, relayTxCost, extraGas?, extraGasFundAmount?, extraGasTxCost?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
  --unsigned: { mode, operation, withdrawMode, chain, transactions[], ... }
  --unsigned tx: [{ from, to, data, value, valueHex, chainId, description }]
  --dry-run: { operation, mode, dryRun, amount, asset, chain, recipient, poolAccountNumber, poolAccountId, selectedCommitmentLabel, selectedCommitmentValue, proofPublicSignals, feeBPS?, quoteExpiresAt?, extraGas?, anonymitySet?: { eligible, total, percentage } }

Additional modes:
  --unsigned builds transaction payloads without submitting.
  --dry-run validates the operation without submitting it.
helpTextByPath.withdraw quote:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools withdraw quote [options] <amountOrAsset> [amount][22m

Request relayer quote and limits without generating a proof

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  [33mamountOrAsset[39m                 Amount to withdraw (or asset symbol, see
                                examples)
  [33mamount[39m                        Amount (when asset is the first argument)


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-a, --asset <symbol|address>[39m  Asset to quote
  [33m-t, --to <address>[39m            Recipient address (recommended for an accurate
                                fee quote)
  [33m-h, --help[39m                    display help for command
Examples:
  privacy-pools withdraw quote 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient...
  privacy-pools withdraw quote 100 USDC --agent --chain mainnet

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init

Agent workflow:
  Quotes expire quickly; submit the withdrawal promptly after quoting if the fee is acceptable. Check runnable=false on nextActions for template commands that still need required user input.
  For agents, prefer `withdraw quote <amount> --asset <symbol|address>` or `withdraw quote <amount> <asset>`. The legacy asset-first positional form also works but is less clear for machine callers.

JSON output (--json):
  { mode: "relayed-quote", chain, asset, amount, recipient, minWithdrawAmount, minWithdrawAmountFormatted, baseFeeBPS, quoteFeeBPS, feeAmount, netAmount, feeCommitmentPresent, quoteExpiresAt, relayTxCost, extraGas?, extraGasFundAmount?, extraGasTxCost?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
helpTextByPath.ragequit:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools ragequit|exit [options] [asset][22m

Publicly recover funds to your original deposit address (self-custody guarantee)

[1m[36mArguments:[39m[22m
  [33masset[39m                         Optional positional asset alias (e.g., ragequit
                                ETH)


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-a, --asset <symbol|address>[39m  Asset pool to ragequit from
  [33m-p, --from-pa <PA-#|#>[39m        Ragequit a specific Pool Account (e.g. PA-2)
  [33m--unsigned [format][39m           Build unsigned transaction without submitting;
                                format: envelope (default) or tx (choices:
                                "envelope", "tx")
  [33m--dry-run[39m                     Generate proof and validate without submitting
  [33m-h, --help[39m                    display help for command
Public recovery path that returns funds to the original deposit address. Does not preserve privacy. Use this for declined, PoA-blocked, or otherwise unrecoverable Pool Accounts, or when you choose not to wait for approval.
Asset lookup still works when live public pool discovery is unavailable because the CLI keeps a built-in onchain-verified registry for supported pools.

Examples:
  privacy-pools ragequit ETH --from-pa PA-1
  privacy-pools ragequit ETH --unsigned --from-pa PA-1
  privacy-pools ragequit ETH --dry-run --from-pa PA-1
  privacy-pools ragequit ETH --from-pa PA-1 --chain mainnet

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init (account state should be synced)

Safety notes:
  Ragequit is public and irreversible and reveals the original deposit address onchain.

Agent workflow:
  If the public recovery transaction was submitted but confirmation timed out, re-run ragequit or sync --chain <chain> before retrying so the CLI can detect the onchain result.

JSON output (--json):
  { operation, txHash, amount, asset, chain, poolAccountNumber, poolAccountId, poolAddress, scope, blockNumber, explorerUrl, destinationAddress?, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
  --unsigned: { mode, operation, chain, asset, amount, transactions[] }
  --unsigned tx: [{ from, to, data, value, valueHex, chainId, description }]
  --dry-run: { dryRun, operation, chain, asset, amount, destinationAddress?, poolAccountNumber, poolAccountId, selectedCommitmentLabel, selectedCommitmentValue, proofPublicSignals, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }

Additional modes:
  --unsigned builds transaction payloads without submitting.
  --dry-run validates the operation without submitting it.
helpTextByPath.accounts:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools accounts [options][22m

List your Pool Accounts with balances


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m--no-sync[39m       Use cached data (faster, but may be stale)
  [33m--all-chains[39m    Include testnet chains (mainnet chains shown by default)
  [33m--details[39m       Show additional details per Pool Account
  [33m--summary[39m       Show counts and balances only
  [33m--pending-only[39m  Show only pending ASP approvals
  [33m-h, --help[39m      display help for command
Shows each Pool Account, its ASP review state, and per-pool aggregate balances. Bare `accounts` is a mainnet dashboard; use --chain for a specific network or --all-chains to include supported testnets.
Compact modes like --summary and --pending-only are intended for agent polling loops so they do not have to parse the full account dataset on every check.

Examples:
  privacy-pools accounts
  privacy-pools accounts --all-chains
  privacy-pools accounts --details
  privacy-pools accounts --summary
  privacy-pools accounts --chain <name> --pending-only
  privacy-pools accounts --agent
  privacy-pools accounts --no-sync --chain mainnet

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init

Agent workflow:
  Without --chain, accounts aggregates all CLI-supported mainnet chains by default. Use --all-chains to include testnets.
  Use --summary or --pending-only to reduce JSON size for polling loops.
  When a Pool Account disappears from --pending-only results, re-run accounts without --pending-only to confirm whether it was approved, declined, or requires Proof of Association (tornado.0xbow.io) before choosing withdraw or ragequit.

JSON output (--json):

  --summary: { chain, allChains?, chains?, warnings?, pendingCount, approvedCount, poaRequiredCount, declinedCount, unknownCount, spentCount, exitedCount, balances, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
  --pending-only: { chain, allChains?, chains?, warnings?, accounts, pendingCount, nextActions?: [{ command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, runnable? }] }
helpTextByPath.migrate:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools migrate [options] [command][22m

Inspect legacy migration readiness on CLI-supported chains


  [1mExplore (no wallet needed)[22m
  [36mstatus [options][39m  Show legacy migration readiness on CLI-supported chains


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m-h, --help[39m        display help for command

Read-only command for legacy pre-upgrade accounts on chains currently supported by the CLI. It rebuilds the legacy account view from the installed SDK plus current onchain events, then reports whether the Privacy Pools website migration flow or website-based recovery is needed.
The CLI does not submit legacy migrations. Use the Privacy Pools website for actual migration or website-based recovery.

Examples:
  privacy-pools migrate status
  privacy-pools migrate status --chain mainnet
  privacy-pools migrate status --all-chains --agent

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init
helpTextByPath.migrate status:
[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools migrate status [options][22m

Show legacy migration readiness on CLI-supported chains


[1m[36mOptions:[39m[22m
  [33m--all-chains[39m  Include testnet chains (mainnet chains shown by default)
  [33m-h, --help[39m    display help for command
Reconstructs the legacy account view without persisting local account state, using the built-in CLI pool registry plus current onchain events for CLI-supported chains, then summarizes whether legacy commitments still need website migration, appear fully migrated already, or require website-based public recovery instead.
Without --chain, migrate status checks all CLI-supported mainnet chains by default. Use --all-chains to include testnets.

Examples:
  privacy-pools migrate status
  privacy-pools migrate status --chain mainnet
  privacy-pools migrate status --all-chains --agent

Prerequisites:
  Requires: init

Safety notes:
  This command is read-only. It never submits migration transactions and does not persist rebuilt account state.
  When readinessResolved is false, treat the result as incomplete and review the account in the Privacy Pools website before acting on it.
  This check is limited to chains currently supported by the CLI. Review beta or other website-only migration surfaces in the Privacy Pools website.

Agent workflow:
  Use this after init/import when the CLI warns that a legacy pre-upgrade account may need website migration or website-based recovery.

JSON output (--json):
  { mode: "migration-status", chain, allChains?, chains?, warnings?, status, requiresMigration, requiresWebsiteRecovery, isFullyMigrated, readinessResolved, submissionSupported: false, requiredChainIds, migratedChainIds, missingChainIds, websiteRecoveryChainIds, unresolvedChainIds, chainReadiness: [{ chain, chainId, status, candidateLegacyCommitments, expectedLegacyCommitments, migratedCommitments, legacyMasterSeedNullifiedCount, hasPostMigrationCommitments, isMigrated, legacySpendableCommitments, upgradedSpendableCommitments, declinedLegacyCommitments, reviewStatusComplete, requiresMigration, requiresWebsiteRecovery, scopes }] }

[1m[36mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools sync [options][22m

Most wallet-aware commands already auto-sync with a 2-minute freshness window, so explicit sync is mainly a crash-recovery or reconciliation tool rather than a command you should need on every workflow step.

guideStructuredText:
Privacy Pools: Quick Guide

Install & Run
  npm i -g privacy-pools-cli
  npm i -g github:0xmatthewb/privacy-pools-cli  (unreleased/source builds)
  privacy-pools status
  privacy-pools upgrade --check                (check npm for a newer installed release)
  npm run dev -- status                        (from source, no global install)
  privacy-pools completion --help                  (shell autocomplete setup)

Quick Start
  privacy-pools init
  privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient          (easy path: deposit now, withdraw later)
  privacy-pools flow start 100 USDC --to 0xRecipient --new-wallet  (easy path with a dedicated workflow wallet)
  privacy-pools pools                                          (browse available pools)
  privacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH
  privacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet --pending-only        (poll ASP review; keep the same --chain until it disappears)
  privacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet                       (then confirm approved vs declined vs POA Needed)
  privacy-pools withdraw 0.05 ETH --to 0xRecipient --from-pa PA-1
  Transaction commands use your default chain (set during init).
  Public dashboards like pools/activity/stats default to all CLI-supported mainnet chains unless you pass --chain.
  Accounts is wallet-dependent: use --chain to keep approval checks on the same network as the deposit.

  Deposits are reviewed by the ASP (Association Set Provider) before approval.
  Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).
  ASP approval is required for withdraw, including --direct. If a deposit is
  declined, use ragequit for public recovery to the original deposit address.
  Declined saved easy-path workflows use 'flow ragequit' as their canonical
  public recovery path, and operators can also choose it manually after the
  public deposit exists.

Two-Key Model
  Privacy Pools uses two keys:
  Recovery phrase  enables private withdrawals (generated during init; sometimes called a seed phrase)
  Signer key       pays gas and sends transactions (can be set later)
  These are independent. You can set the signer key later via env var.
  Note: PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY env var takes precedence over a saved key file.
  Exception: flow start --new-wallet creates and uses a dedicated per-workflow wallet instead of the configured signer.

Workflow
  1. init           Set up wallet and config (run once)
  2. flow start     Easy path: deposit now and save a later private withdrawal
  3. flow watch     Resume a saved workflow through funding, approval, delay, and withdrawal
  4. flow ragequit  Saved-workflow public recovery if you stop waiting or the easy path is declined
  5. pools          Manual path: browse available pools
  6. deposit        Manual path: deposit into a pool (vetting fee shown before confirming)
  7. accounts       Manual path: poll pending review, then confirm approval status and balances
  8. migrate status Read-only legacy check on CLI-supported chains
  9. withdraw       Manual path: withdraw privately (once approved; fee shown before confirming)
 10. history        View transaction history
  *  status         Check setup and connection health (checks run by default)
  *  upgrade        Check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI
  *  activity       Public onchain feed (for your history, use 'history')
  *  ragequit       Public withdrawal. Returns funds to deposit address (alias: exit)
  *  withdraw quote Check relayer fees before withdrawing
  'migrate status' is read-only. The CLI does not submit migration transactions; use the website for actual migration or website-based recovery.
  It only checks chains currently supported by the CLI; review beta or website-only legacy migration surfaces in the website.
  'flow start --new-wallet' generates a dedicated workflow wallet and waits for funding automatically.
  In machine mode, this path requires '--export-new-wallet <path>' so the generated key is backed up first.
  Manual commands remain available for advanced control.

Global Options
  -c, --chain <name>    Target chain (mainnet, arbitrum, optimism; testnets: sepolia, op-sepolia)
  -r, --rpc-url <url>   Override RPC URL
  -j, --json            Machine-readable JSON output
  --format <fmt>        Output format: table (default), csv, json
  --no-color            Disable colored output (also respects NO_COLOR env var)
  -y, --yes             Skip confirmation prompts
  -q, --quiet           Suppress human-oriented stderr output
  -v, --verbose         Enable verbose/debug output
  --agent               Alias for --json --yes --quiet (agent/automation mode)
  --timeout <seconds>  Network/transaction timeout (default: 30)
  --no-banner           Disable ASCII banner

Environment Variables
  PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY   Signer key (takes precedence over saved signer key file)
  PRIVACY_POOLS_HOME / PRIVACY_POOLS_CONFIG_DIR  Config directory override (default: ~/.privacy-pools)
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL / PP_RPC_URL             Override RPC endpoint for all chains
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST / PP_ASP_HOST           Override ASP endpoint for all chains
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST / PP_RELAYER_HOST   Override relayer endpoint for all chains
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL_<CHAIN> / PP_RPC_URL_<CHAIN>         Override RPC endpoint per chain
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN>       Override ASP endpoint per chain
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> Override relayer endpoint per chain
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CIRCUITS_DIR   Override the circuit artifact directory
  NO_COLOR                     Disable colored output (same as --no-color)
  PP_NO_UPDATE_CHECK           Set to 1 to disable the update-available notification

Interaction Modes
  Human mode (default): interactive prompts + readable output.
  Agent mode: --agent for structured JSON output, no prompts, no banners.
  Equivalent to --json --yes --quiet.

Advanced Modes
  Unsigned mode builds transaction payloads without signing or submitting.
  Requires init (recovery phrase) for deposit secret generation.
  Does NOT require a signer key. The signing party provides their own.
  Output includes from plus description. from is null when the signer is unconstrained, and set when the protocol requires a specific caller.
  --unsigned           (default) Wrapped in JSON envelope: { schemaVersion, success, ... }
  --unsigned tx        Raw transaction array: [{ from, to, data, value, valueHex, chainId, description }]
             Raw format skips the envelope. Intended for direct piping to signing tools.
  --dry-run    Validate and generate proofs without submitting.

Troubleshooting
  Stale data?      Commands auto-sync; force a full re-sync with 'privacy-pools sync'.
  ASP unreachable?  Check 'privacy-pools status' (health checks run by default).
  Long proof time?  Proofs use bundled circuit artifacts. The first proof may spend a moment verifying them.
  Native fallback?  Set PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 to force the JS launcher,
                   or see docs/runtime-upgrades.md for runtime troubleshooting and overrides.
  Upgrade path?     Run privacy-pools upgrade to check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI.
  Not approved?     Deposits are reviewed by the ASP. Most deposits are
                   approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer
                   (up to 7 days). Some may require Proof of Association
                   or be declined. Declined deposits must use ragequit
                   for public recovery.
  Custom RPC?       Pass --rpc-url on any command, or save per-chain overrides in
                   ~/.privacy-pools/config.json under "rpcOverrides": { "<chainId>": "https://..." }.

Exit Codes
  0  Success
  1  Unknown/general error
  2  Input/validation error
  3  RPC/network error
  4  ASP (Association Set Provider) error
  5  Relayer error
  6  Proof generation error
  7  Contract revert

Using CLI with an Existing Website Account
  If you already use privacypools.com, you can access the same account from the CLI:
  1. Export your 12/24-word recovery phrase from the website.
  2. Run: privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file ./recovery.txt (or: cat recovery.txt | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-stdin)
  3. Set your signer key: export PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY=0x...
  4. Run: privacy-pools accounts  (syncs on-chain state automatically)
  If the website account was created before the 2024 security update, run privacy-pools migrate status to inspect legacy readiness on CLI-supported chains. Review beta or website-only legacy migration surfaces in the website.

Terminology
  Recovery phrase          24-word mnemonic by default in the CLI; imports may be 12/24 words.
  Signer key               Private key that pays gas and sends transactions.
  Pool Account (PA)        A single deposit and its balance, tracked for withdrawal or exit.
  ASP (Association Set Provider) status  approved (withdraw ready), pending (waiting),
                                   poa_required (Proof of Association needed),
                                   declined (ragequit only), unknown (unresolved).
  Relayed withdrawal       Privacy-preserving withdrawal via a relayer (recommended).
  Direct withdrawal        Non-private withdrawal; links deposit and withdrawal onchain.
  Ragequit (exit alias)    Public, irreversible withdrawal to original deposit address.

Agent Integration
  For programmatic/agent use, run privacy-pools capabilities --agent to discover
  commands, schemas, supported chains, error codes, and the recommended workflow.
  Use privacy-pools describe <command...> --agent to inspect one command at runtime.

Further Reading
  privacy-pools <command> --help  (command-specific details and examples)
  Package-relative docs (open from a source checkout or installed package root):
  docs/reference.md   Flags, configuration, environment variables, project structure
  docs/runtime-upgrades.md  Native runtime troubleshooting, fallback controls, upgrade playbook
  AGENTS.md           Agent integration guide, JSON payloads, unsigned mode
  CHANGELOG.md        Release history and migration notes

  Run privacy-pools <command> --help for command-specific details.

[1m[36mQuick Start:[39m[22m

  [36mprivacy-pools flow start 100 USDC --to 0xRecipient --new-wallet[39m  [2m(easy path with a dedicated workflow wallet)[22m

  [36mprivacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH[39m

[2m  Transaction commands use your default chain (set during init).[22m
[2m  Public dashboards like pools/activity/stats default to all CLI-supported mainnet chains unless you pass --chain.[22m
[2m  Accounts is wallet-dependent: use --chain to keep approval checks on the same network as the deposit.[22m

[2m  Deposits are reviewed by the ASP (Association Set Provider) before approval.[22m
[2m  Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).[22m
[2m  ASP approval is required for withdraw, including --direct. If a deposit is[22m
[2m  declined, use ragequit for public recovery to the original deposit address.[22m
[2m  Declined saved easy-path workflows use 'flow ragequit' as their canonical[22m
[2m  public recovery path, and operators can also choose it manually after the[22m
[2m  public deposit exists.[22m

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mTroubleshooting:[39m[22m
  Stale data?      Commands auto-sync; force a full re-sync with 'privacy-pools sync'.
  ASP unreachable?  Check 'privacy-pools status' (health checks run by default).
  Long proof time?  Proofs use bundled circuit artifacts. The first proof may spend a moment verifying them.
  Native fallback?  Set PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 to force the JS launcher,
                   or see docs/runtime-upgrades.md for runtime troubleshooting and overrides.
  Upgrade path?     Run [36mprivacy-pools upgrade[39m to check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI.
  Not approved?     Deposits are reviewed by the ASP. Most deposits are
                   approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer
                   (up to 7 days). Some may require Proof of Association
                   or be declined. Declined deposits must use ragequit
                   for public recovery.
  Custom RPC?       Pass --rpc-url on any command, or save per-chain overrides in
                   ~/.privacy-pools/config.json under [2m"rpcOverrides": { "<chainId>": "https://..." }[22m.

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mExit Codes:[39m[22m
  [32m0[39m  Success
  [91m1[39m  Unknown/general error
  [91m2[39m  Input/validation error
  [91m3[39m  RPC/network error
  [91m4[39m  ASP (Association Set Provider) error
  [91m5[39m  Relayer error
  [91m6[39m  Proof generation error
  [91m7[39m  Contract revert

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mUsing CLI with an Existing Website Account:[39m[22m
  If you already use [36mprivacypools.com[39m, you can access the same account from the CLI:
  1. Export your 12/24-word recovery phrase from the website.
  2. Run: [36mprivacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file ./recovery.txt[39m [2m(or: cat recovery.txt | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-stdin)[22m
  3. Set your signer key: [36mexport PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY=0x...[39m
  4. Run: [36mprivacy-pools accounts[39m  [2m(syncs on-chain state automatically)[22m
  If the website account was created before the 2024 security update, run [36mprivacy-pools migrate status[39m to inspect legacy readiness on CLI-supported chains. Review beta or website-only legacy migration surfaces in the website.

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mTerminology:[39m[22m
  [33mRecovery phrase[39m          24-word mnemonic by default in the CLI; imports may be 12/24 words.
  [33mSigner key[39m               Private key that pays gas and sends transactions.
  [33mPool Account (PA)[39m        A single deposit and its balance, tracked for withdrawal or exit.
  [33mASP (Association Set Provider) status[39m  [32mapproved[39m (withdraw ready), [33mpending[39m (waiting),
                                   [33mpoa_required[39m (Proof of Association needed),
                                   [91mdeclined[39m (ragequit only), [2munknown[22m (unresolved).
  [33mRelayed withdrawal[39m       Privacy-preserving withdrawal via a relayer (recommended).
  [33mDirect withdrawal[39m        Non-private withdrawal; links deposit and withdrawal onchain.
  [33mRagequit (exit alias)[39m    Public, irreversible withdrawal to original deposit address.

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mAgent Integration:[39m[22m
  For programmatic/agent use, run [36mprivacy-pools capabilities --agent[39m to discover
  commands, schemas, supported chains, error codes, and the recommended workflow.
  Use [36mprivacy-pools describe <command...> --agent[39m to inspect one command at runtime.

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m

[1m[36mPrivacy Pools CLI: Agent Capabilities[39m[22m

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mCommands:[39m[22m
  init: Initialize wallet and configuration
    Agent usage: privacy-pools init --agent --default-chain <chain> --show-recovery-phrase
  upgrade: Check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI
    Agent usage: privacy-pools upgrade --agent [--check] [--yes]
  flow: Guided deposit-to-private-withdrawal workflow
    Agent usage: privacy-pools flow start <amount> <asset> --to <address> [--privacy-delay <profile>] --agent (or: watch [workflowId|latest] [--privacy-delay <profile>] --agent; status/ragequit --agent)
  flow start: Deposit now and save a later private withdrawal workflow
    Agent usage: privacy-pools flow start <amount> <asset> --to <address> --agent [--privacy-delay <profile>] [--watch] [--new-wallet] [--export-new-wallet <path>]
  flow watch: Resume a saved flow through funding, approval, privacy delay, and withdrawal
    Agent usage: privacy-pools flow watch [workflowId|latest] --agent [--privacy-delay <profile>]
  flow status: Show the saved easy-path workflow state
    Agent usage: privacy-pools flow status [workflowId|latest] --agent
  flow ragequit: Recover a saved workflow publicly via ragequit
    Agent usage: privacy-pools flow ragequit [workflowId|latest] --agent
  pools: List available pools and assets
    Agent usage: privacy-pools pools --agent [--all-chains] [--search <query>] [--sort <mode>]
  activity: Show public activity feed
    Agent usage: privacy-pools activity --agent [--asset <symbol>] [--page <n>] [--limit <n>]
  stats: Show public statistics
    Agent usage: privacy-pools stats global --agent (or: pool --asset <symbol> --agent)
  stats global: Show global Privacy Pools statistics (all-time and last 24h)
    Agent usage: privacy-pools stats global --agent
  stats pool: Show statistics for a specific pool (all-time and last 24h)
    Agent usage: privacy-pools stats pool --asset <symbol|address> --agent
  describe: Describe one command for runtime agent introspection
    Agent usage: privacy-pools describe <command...> --agent
  deposit: Deposit funds into a Privacy Pool
    Agent usage: privacy-pools deposit <amount> [asset] --agent
  withdraw: Privately withdraw funds via relayer
    Agent usage: privacy-pools withdraw [amount] [asset] --to <address> --agent
  withdraw quote: Request relayer quote and limits without generating a proof
    Agent usage: privacy-pools withdraw quote <amount> --asset <symbol|address> --agent
  accounts: List your Pool Accounts with balances
    Agent usage: privacy-pools accounts --agent
  migrate: Inspect legacy migration readiness on CLI-supported chains
    Agent usage: privacy-pools migrate status --agent [--all-chains]
  migrate status: Show legacy migration readiness on CLI-supported chains
    Agent usage: privacy-pools migrate status --agent [--all-chains]
  history: Show chronological event history (deposits, migrations, withdrawals, ragequits)
    Agent usage: privacy-pools history --agent
  sync: Force-sync local account state from onchain events
    Agent usage: privacy-pools sync --agent [--asset <symbol>]
  status: Show configuration and check connection health
    Agent usage: privacy-pools status --agent [--no-check] [--check-rpc] [--check-asp]
  ragequit (alias: exit): Publicly recover funds to your original deposit address (self-custody guarantee)
    Agent usage: privacy-pools ragequit [asset] --from-pa <PA-#> --agent
  guide: Show usage guide, workflow, and reference
    Agent usage: privacy-pools guide --agent
  completion: Generate shell completion script
    Agent usage: privacy-pools completion --agent <shell>
  capabilities: Describe CLI capabilities for agent discovery
    Agent usage: privacy-pools capabilities --agent

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mGlobal flags:[39m[22m
  -c, --chain <name>: Target chain (mainnet, arbitrum, optimism, ...)
  -j, --json: Machine-readable JSON output on stdout
  --format <format>: Output format: table (default), csv, json
  -y, --yes: Skip confirmation prompts
  -r, --rpc-url <url>: Override RPC URL
  --agent: Machine-friendly mode (alias for --json --yes --quiet)
  -q, --quiet: Suppress human-oriented stderr output
  --no-banner: Disable ASCII banner output
  -v, --verbose: Enable verbose/debug output
  --timeout <seconds>: Network/transaction timeout in seconds (default: 30)
  --no-color: Disable colored output (also respects NO_COLOR env var)

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mExit codes:[39m[22m
  0 SUCCESS (SUCCESS): Successful command completion.
  1 UNKNOWN (UNKNOWN_ERROR): Unknown or general runtime failure.
  2 INPUT (INPUT_ERROR): Invalid input, prompt cancellation in machine mode, or validation failure.
  3 RPC (RPC_ERROR): RPC, transport, or network connectivity failure.
  4 ASP (ASP_ERROR): ASP service failure or approval-state fetch issue.
  5 RELAYER (RELAYER_ERROR): Relayer quote or submission failure.
  6 PROOF (PROOF_ERROR): ZK proof generation or proof-input failure.
  7 CONTRACT (CONTRACT_ERROR): Onchain simulation or contract revert failure.

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mEnvironment variables:[39m[22m
  PRIVACY_POOLS_HOME (aliases: PRIVACY_POOLS_CONFIG_DIR): Override the CLI config directory.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY: Signer private key; takes precedence over the saved .signer file.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL (aliases: PP_RPC_URL): Override the RPC endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST (aliases: PP_ASP_HOST): Override the ASP endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST (aliases: PP_RELAYER_HOST): Override the relayer endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CIRCUITS_DIR: Override the circuit artifact directory with a trusted pre-provisioned path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL_<CHAIN> (aliases: PP_RPC_URL_<CHAIN>): Override the RPC endpoint for one chain, for example ARBITRUM or SEPOLIA.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN> (aliases: PP_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN>): Override the ASP endpoint for one chain.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> (aliases: PP_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN>): Override the relayer endpoint for one chain.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_DISABLE_NATIVE: Set to 1 to force the pure JS runtime path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_BINARY: Advanced maintainer override for the launcher target native-shell binary path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_JS_WORKER: Advanced maintainer override for the packaged JS worker entrypoint.
  NO_COLOR: Disable colored output, matching --no-color.
  PP_NO_UPDATE_CHECK: Set to 1 to disable the update-available notification.

[1m[36mTypical agent workflow:[39m[22m
  1. privacy-pools status --agent
  2. privacy-pools init --agent --default-chain <chain> --show-recovery-phrase
  3. privacy-pools pools --agent --chain <chain>
  4. privacy-pools flow start <amount> <asset> --to <address> --agent --chain <chain>
  5. privacy-pools flow watch [workflowId|latest] --agent
  6. privacy-pools flow ragequit [workflowId|latest] --agent  (optional public recovery after the deposit exists; canonical if the saved workflow is declined)
  7. privacy-pools deposit <amount> --asset <symbol> --agent --chain <chain>  (manual alternative)
  8. privacy-pools accounts --agent --chain <chain> --pending-only  (reviewed entries disappear; confirm approved vs declined vs poa_required with accounts --agent --chain <chain>)
  9. privacy-pools withdraw <amount> --asset <symbol> --to <address> --agent --chain <chain>

[1m[36mSafety notes:[39m[22m
  The recovery phrase and signer key are independent secrets: the phrase controls deposit privacy, the key pays gas. Neither is derived from the other.
  Newly generated recovery phrases use 24 words for stronger security. Imported recovery phrases may still be 12 or 24 words.
  Legacy pre-upgrade accounts may need website migration or website-based recovery before the CLI can safely restore them.

[1m[36mAgent workflow:[39m[22m
  When generating a new recovery phrase in machine mode, pass --show-recovery-phrase and capture it immediately.
  When importing an existing recovery phrase, nextActions points to migrate status --agent --all-chains first to check for existing deposits across all chains before transacting.

[1m[36mSafety notes:[39m[22m
  Automatic upgrade only runs for recognized global npm installs of privacy-pools-cli.
  Source checkouts, non-npm global installs, local project installs, npx-style ephemeral runs, CI, and ambiguous contexts stay read-only and still return an exact npm follow-up command.
  A successful upgrade updates the installed CLI on disk but does not hot-reexec the current process. Re-run privacy-pools after it completes.

[1m[36mSafety notes:[39m[22m
  Deposits are always public on-chain. The ASP reviews the deposit before private withdrawal is possible.
  If --to is omitted in interactive mode, the CLI prompts for the recipient. In machine modes, --to remains required.
  In machine modes, non-round flow amounts are rejected. Use a round amount in agent/non-interactive runs, or switch to interactive mode if you intentionally accept that tradeoff.
  New workflows default to a balanced post-approval privacy delay before relayed withdrawal. off = no added hold, balanced = randomized 15 to 90 minutes, aggressive = randomized 2 to 12 hours.
  Vetting fees can turn a round deposit input into a non-round committed balance, so flow start may still emit an advisory amount-pattern warning for the later full-balance auto-withdrawal.
  flow start surfaces advisory privacy warnings when the saved workflow is configured to auto-withdraw a full non-round balance, or when timing delay is explicitly disabled.
  --export-new-wallet is only valid with --new-wallet.
  Non-interactive workflow wallets require --export-new-wallet so the generated private key is backed up before the flow starts.
  The generated workflow key is also stored locally under workflow-secrets until the workflow completes or recovers publicly, so --export-new-wallet is a backup copy rather than the only retained secret.
  Dedicated workflow wallets may retain leftover asset balance or gas reserve after paused or terminal states, so check them manually before assuming they are empty.
  The saved flow spends the entire remaining Pool Account balance, but the recipient receives the net amount after relayer fees and any ERC20 extra-gas funding.
  Manual commands remain the advanced/manual path when you need custom control over Pool Account selection, amount, or withdrawal mode.

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mAgent workflow:[39m[22m
  With --new-wallet, the flow stays attached automatically and waits for funding, deposit, approval, and withdrawal unless you detach with Ctrl-C.
  Use --watch to stay attached on configured-wallet workflows; otherwise the workflow is persisted locally and flow watch <workflowId> is the canonical resume path.

[1m[36mSafety notes:[39m[22m
  Paused states are successful workflow states, not CLI errors. Declined workflows surface flow ragequit as the canonical public recovery path, and PoA-required workflows can either resume privately after the external Proof of Association step or recover publicly with flow ragequit.
  If the saved full-balance withdrawal falls below the relayer minimum, flow watch surfaces flow ragequit as the required public recovery path because saved flows only support relayed private withdrawals.
  Once the public deposit exists, operators can also choose flow ragequit manually instead of waiting, but it is not emitted as the default nextAction while the workflow is still progressing normally. The happy-path canonical resume command remains flow watch.
  Passing --privacy-delay on flow watch updates the saved workflow policy. off = no added hold, balanced = randomized 15 to 90 minutes, aggressive = randomized 2 to 12 hours.
  Switching to off clears any saved hold immediately; switching between balanced and aggressive resamples from the override time.

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mAgent workflow:[39m[22m
  New-wallet workflows wait for funding automatically. ERC20 workflows require both the token amount and a native ETH gas reserve in the generated wallet before the public deposit can proceed.
  When the saved Pool Account is approved, flow watch performs the relayed private withdrawal automatically using the saved recipient and the full remaining balance of that same Pool Account after any configured privacy delay hold expires.
  flow watch keeps polling until the saved workflow changes or finishes. If your automation should stop after a fixed duration, wrap the CLI call in your own external timeout.

[1m[36mSafety notes:[39m[22m
  Deposits are reviewed by the ASP before approval. Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).
  A vetting fee is deducted from the deposit amount by the pool's ASP.
  Only approved deposits can use withdraw, whether relayed or direct. Declined deposits must use ragequit publicly. Deposits that require Proof of Association (PoA) must complete the PoA flow at tornado.0xbow.io before they can withdraw privately.

[2mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[22m
[1m[36mAgent workflow:[39m[22m
  Poll accounts --chain <chain> --pending-only while the Pool Account remains pending; when it disappears from pending results, re-run accounts --chain <chain> to confirm whether aspStatus became approved, declined, or requires Proof of Association. Withdraw only after approval; ragequit if declined; complete Proof of Association at tornado.0xbow.io first if needed. Always preserve the same --chain scope for both polling and confirmation.
  If the deposit transaction was submitted but confirmation timed out or the CLI was interrupted afterward, run sync --chain <chain> before retrying so local state can detect the onchain deposit.
